title: Package version is deprecated
trigger: Manifest deprecated metadata is set.

title: Runs lifecycle scripts during install
trigger: preinstall, install, postinstall, prepublish, or prepare script is present.

title: Exposes executable entrypoints
trigger: Package manifest exposes one or more bin entries.

title: Very new package
trigger: Package was created less than 14 days ago.

title: Young package
trigger: Package was created less than 90 days ago.

title: Freshly published version
trigger: Resolved version was published less than 24 hours ago.

title: No license metadata
trigger: Manifest and packument do not declare license metadata.

title: No repository metadata
trigger: Manifest and packument do not link to source code.

title: Large direct dependency fan-out
trigger: Direct plus optional dependency count is greater than 60.

title: Single maintainer
trigger: Package has one or fewer maintainers.

title: Downloads remote code or data
trigger: A bin or lifecycle target preview references curl, wget, Invoke-WebRequest, fetch, axios, http.get, or https.get.

title: Spawns shell commands
trigger: A bin or lifecycle target preview references child_process, exec, execSync, execFileSync, spawn, or spawnSync.

title: Uses dynamic code evaluation
trigger: A bin or lifecycle target preview references eval, new Function, vm.runIn, or WebAssembly.compile.

title: References local secrets
trigger: A bin or lifecycle target preview references .npmrc, .ssh, common token names, or process.env.

title: Touches home or system paths
trigger: A bin or lifecycle target preview references homedir(), /etc/, ~/, APPDATA, or LOCALAPPDATA.

title: Contains encoded payload markers
trigger: A bin or lifecycle target preview contains base64 decode markers, fromCharCode, or long base64-like runs.

title: Decoded payload contains sensitive code
trigger: Bounded base64 decoding reveals shell, network, eval, secret, or filesystem access patterns.

title: Entrypoint uses install/run-sensitive code
trigger: A bin or lifecycle target file uses shell, network, or environment access patterns.

title: Many nested package manifests
trigger: Published tarball contains more than three package.json files.

title: Large tarball
trigger: Tarball download exceeds the configured max tarball byte cap.

title: Large unpacked package
trigger: Manifest reports unpacked size greater than four times the tarball byte cap.

title: Risky dependency in resolved tree
trigger: Bounded dependency analysis finds a dependency scoring at least 25/100.

title: Dependency resolves outside the npm registry
trigger: Dependency graph contains GitHub, tarball URL, local, workspace, link, arbitrary git, or unsupported package specs.

title: Dependency install target uses sensitive code
trigger: A fetched dependency tarball contains lifecycle/bin-sensitive behavior or suspicious decoded payloads.

title: Known security advisory affects this package
trigger: Injected advisory source reports one or more advisories for the resolved package version.

title: Package name resembles a popular package
trigger: Package base name is within the allowed edit distance of a popular package name.

title: Maintainer reputation needs review
trigger: Maintainer account age, 2FA, or recent ownership-change metadata suggests elevated takeover risk.

title: Expected provenance attestation is missing
trigger: Package metadata or install-capable behavior expects provenance, but no attestation is present.

title: New lifecycle script in this version
trigger: Current version adds or changes an install-time lifecycle script compared with the previous semver version.

title: New or changed executable entrypoint
trigger: Current version adds a bin entrypoint or changes an existing bin target compared with the previous semver version.

title: Direct dependency count spiked
trigger: Direct plus optional dependency count increased by at least 10, or doubled from a previous count of at least 5.

title: New install-capable dependency
trigger: Current version adds a direct or optional dependency with lifecycle scripts or executable bins.

title: Tarball size or file count spiked
trigger: Reported unpacked size or file count increased sharply compared with the previous semver version.

title: New sensitive entrypoint behavior
trigger: A bin or lifecycle target became sensitive compared with the previous version's tarball.

title: New obfuscated file behavior
trigger: Current tarball adds file content that decodes an obfuscated payload and executes or writes it.
